summary: v1.3.2 iteration-8 RETEST (focused on iteration-7 unresolved items). FALL-DETECTION CRASH IS FIXED: /fall-detection-test now mounts on Expo web with no 'addListener is not a function' error. The module-load Platform.OS gate in src/fallDetector.ts (lines 85-101) correctly substitutes no-op stubs for Accelerometer + Gyroscope on web, so the useFallDetector subscription effect cannot reach a native sensor. fall-test-phase renders '⚪ Idle — waiting for impact', accelerometer shows 'NOT AVAILABLE'. The global FallDetectionOverlay also mounts on every screen and is functional (verified by invoking the documented globalThis.__kc_triggerFall test hook — modal appears with fall-modal/fall-countdown '29s remaining'/fall-cancel and cancels cleanly). MEMBER-DETAIL + ADD-ROUTINE flow PASSES end-to-end when reached via in-app tap navigation (not direct location.assign — see context). Tapping the dashboard MemberCard → /member/{id} renders member-refresh-location ('Refresh' chip), member-refreshing-banner (because Refresh-All from dashboard was already in-flight); tapping the chip again keeps the banner up and the chip text reads 'Refreshing…' within <1s. add-routine-btn is visible on the member screen and routes to /add-routine/{id}, which exposes routine-title, routine-time-picker (canonical TimePicker12, NO old 8-block preset grid), and add-routine-submit. POST /api/reminders → HTTP 200 with body {id, title:'Read 20 min', category:'routine', times:[{time:'10:00'}]}. DASHBOARD REGRESSION still passes (refresh-all-btn → per-member 'Refreshing…' indicator; SOS button → 'Are you sure?' confirmation modal with 'Yes, Call 911' + Cancel). One minor expectation gap: tapping fall-test-simulate fires the LOCAL test-page callback (event log shows 'FALL CONFIRMED') but does NOT raise the global FallDetectionOverlay — each useFallDetector instance owns its own simulateFall, so the test-page button only logs locally. Triggering the global overlay requires the existing __kc_triggerFall web hook (or a real impact on native). This is by-design but contradicts the iteration-8 request wording ('Tap Simulate → verify FallDetectionOverlay appears'); flagging for the main agent to decide whether to wire the test-page Simulate into the overlay too.

integration_issues.issue: Tapping testID `fall-test-simulate` on /fall-detection-test fires the LOCAL hook's onFallDetected (event log line '🚨 FALL CONFIRMED — overlay would show 30 s countdown now' appears) but does NOT raise the global FallDetectionOverlay (no fall-modal renders). Reason: useFallDetector returns a per-instance simulateFall; the test page subscribes to its own callback, and the overlay (mounted in app/_layout.tsx) has a separate subscription that is only fired by an actual accelerometer impact (impossible on web) or by globalThis.__kc_triggerFall(). The global hook itself works — invoking globalThis.__kc_triggerFall() shows the modal with countdown '29s remaining' and the I'm okay/cancel + Call 911 buttons. Decide: either (a) accept the existing test-page semantics ('Simulate' = run the algorithm hook end-to-end in isolation; the overlay is verified separately) and update the iteration-8 acceptance wording, or (b) have the test page's Simulate also call globalThis.__kc_triggerFall?.() to fan the trigger into the overlay.

action_items: Confirmed PASS — fallDetector.ts module-load gate eliminates the Expo web red-screen. iteration-7 critical action item is now CLOSED.

Confirmed PASS — /member/{id} (member-refresh-location, member-refreshing-banner) and /add-routine/{memberId} (routine-title, routine-time-picker, add-routine-submit) all mount and the end-to-end POST /api/reminders returns 200.

Minor (decide-and-do): make the test-page Simulate button also fan to the global overlay so the iteration-8 acceptance criterion ('Simulate → overlay appears') is satisfied via UI alone. Two-line change in app/fall-detection-test.tsx: after simulateFall(), call `(globalThis as any).__kc_triggerFall?.()` on web. OR update the test plan to use the existing globalThis.__kc_triggerFall hook to assert the overlay.

Pre-existing dev warning (not introduced this iteration): expo-router prints REPLACE warning 'The action REPLACE with payload (tabs)/dashboard was not handled' on first nav. Harmless in dev; gone in production per the warning message.
critical_code_review_comments: src/fallDetector.ts:85-101 — module-load Platform.OS gate is the correct, deterministic fix. The web bundle now contains a true stub object (isAvailableAsync returns false, setUpdateInterval is no-op, addListener returns {remove:noop}) so no possible call path can reach the native NativeEventEmitter. Hot-reload, StrictMode double-invoke, and async race conditions are all unable to break the gate.

Expo Router web preview note for future testers: direct navigation via `page.goto('/fall-detection-test')` or `location.assign('/fall-detection-test')` will REDIRECT to /dashboard because the SPA root's auth/onboarding routing replaces the requested route. The reliable path is in-app navigation: land on /, click ⚙️ (Settings), scroll to 'Test Fall Detection' row, tap. Same applies to /member/{id} and /add-routine/{id} — must navigate via the dashboard MemberCard tap.

FallDetectionOverlay subscribes per-instance via its OWN useFallDetector — verified working via globalThis.__kc_triggerFall on web. The cancel button (testID fall-cancel) and countdown (testID fall-countdown showing '29s remaining' immediately after trigger) work as designed.

success_rate.backend: n/a (no backend regressions retested this iteration; POST /api/members and POST /api/reminders both returned 200 during the UI flow)
success_rate.frontend: Fall-detection-test page = PASS (no crash, testIDs mount, accelerometer NOT AVAILABLE as expected). Global FallDetectionOverlay = PASS (modal + countdown + cancel verified via __kc_triggerFall). Member detail = PASS (chip + refreshing banner). Add-routine = PASS (testIDs + POST 200). Dashboard regression = PASS (refresh-all + SOS modal). One semantic gap: fall-test-simulate doesn't raise the global overlay (see integration_issues).

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_MomRetest (id 92f9d30b-66dc-4d52-8369-c9a0466f77ae) under Alice's family_group_id, then a routine reminder (id c79c2d26-f497-4117-83fb-46e8c134a3f0). Both deleted via DELETE /api/reminders/{id} (200) and DELETE /api/members/{id} (200). DB is clean.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Web-preview deep-link gotcha: Expo Router's root SPA REDIRECTS direct page.goto('/route') or location.assign('/route') to /dashboard for authenticated users. ALWAYS navigate via in-app taps (click ⚙️ → 'Test Fall Detection' for the test page; click MemberCard → 'Add Routine' for /add-routine). Console error 'REPLACE with payload (tabs)/dashboard was not handled' is a benign dev-only warning and unrelated to user-facing routing. The FallDetectionOverlay can be triggered from Playwright via `await page.evaluate('globalThis.__kc_triggerFall()')` to assert the 30s countdown modal — this hook is installed on web only by FallDetectionOverlay.tsx:121-129. The /fall-detection-test page's Simulate button DOES NOT fan into the global overlay (separate hook instances); it only fires the local test-page log.
rca_of_issue: iteration-7 fall-detection RCA confirmed correct: gating at the call-site (`if (Platform.OS === 'web') return;` inside the subscription useEffect) was insufficient because (a) React 18 strict-mode double-invokes effects on dev and (b) an async-race between isFallEnabled and isFallAvailable could briefly set enabled=true before available was committed false, allowing one pass through the gate that touched Accelerometer.setUpdateInterval + Accelerometer.addListener. The iteration-8 fix moves the gate to the import boundary (`if (Platform.OS === 'web') Accelerometer = stub` at module load), so the served bundle's Accelerometer/Gyroscope symbols are literally no-op stubs on web — there is no possible execution path that can reach a real DeviceSensor.addListener. Verified via live load of /fall-detection-test on the public Expo web preview: page mounts, testIDs render, accelerometer shows 'NOT AVAILABLE', and no JS error is thrown.
